skill.name: ai-wrapper-product
skill.description: Guides teams building focused products around AI APIs such as OpenAI and Anthropic. Covers product architecture, prompt design, model choice, cost controls, usage limits, output validation, and differentiation for AI SaaS.
skill.summary: Plan practical AI API products with prompts, cost controls, validation, and product differentiation.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static findings were reviewed against SKILL.md and were not confirmed as security issues. The external command alerts are Markdown code fences and JavaScript template literals, while the weak crypto and reconnaissance alerts are documentation text false positives. No prompt injection attempt, malicious instruction, credential access, filesystem mutation, or data exfiltration behavior was found.

content.value_statement: Many AI wrapper products fail because they are generic and expensive to operate. This skill helps teams design focused AI products with prompt systems, cost controls, validation, and clear differentiation.
actual_capabilities: Maps an AI product flow from user input through validation, prompting, API calls, parsing, and response delivery.
Provides prompt template patterns for product features with role, format, tone, and input fields.
Explains model selection tradeoffs across cost, speed, quality, and usage volume.
Shows how to track token usage and estimate API cost per request.
Recommends usage limits, output validation, retries, fallback models, and post-processing.
Identifies common AI wrapper anti-patterns and practical alternatives.
limitations: It provides architecture guidance and examples, not a complete production application.
Model pricing and model names may need updates before implementation.
Database calls, billing systems, and API clients are illustrative and must be adapted.
It does not replace legal, privacy, or compliance review for AI products.

use_cases.title: Design a New AI SaaS Feature
use_cases.description: Turn a product idea into a workflow with input validation, prompt templates, model choice, and output handling.

use_cases.title: Control AI API Operating Costs
use_cases.description: Plan usage tracking, token budgeting, cheaper model routing, cache opportunities, and monthly customer limits.

use_cases.title: Improve AI Output Reliability
use_cases.description: Add structured outputs, parsing, validation, retry behavior, and fallback paths to reduce product failures.

prompt_templates.title: Validate an AI Product Idea
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner planning a focused AI product
prompt_templates.prompt: Use ai-wrapper-product to evaluate this AI product idea: [idea]. Identify the target user, core workflow, AI role, differentiation, and main risks.

prompt_templates.title: Design the Wrapper Flow
prompt_templates.prompt: Use ai-wrapper-product to design the end-to-end wrapper flow for [feature]. Include input validation, prompt context, model choice, output parsing, and user response.

prompt_templates.title: Plan Cost Controls
prompt_templates.prompt: Use ai-wrapper-product to create a cost-control plan for [product]. Include token tracking, model routing, caching, limits, and pricing implications.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Product Defensibility
prompt_templates.prompt: Use ai-wrapper-product to audit [AI product] for thin-wrapper risk, workflow fit, output quality controls, rate-limit handling, and defensible advantages.

output_examples.input: Plan an AI email-writing product for sales teams.
output_examples.output: A focused workflow that collects buyer context, deal stage, tone, and call notes; generates a draft; validates length and missing placeholders; and tracks cost per account.

output_examples.input: Reduce costs for a high-volume AI summarization feature.
output: Route short, low-risk summaries to a cheaper model.
Limit output length by plan tier.
Log input tokens, output tokens, model, user, and estimated cost for each request.

output_examples.input: Improve reliability for structured AI responses.
output_examples.output: A plan using strict output instructions, schema validation, retry prompts, fallback models, and user-friendly error messages when parsing fails.

best_practices: Define the user workflow before choosing the model or prompt format.
Track token usage and estimated cost from the first prototype.
Validate AI outputs before showing them or storing them in product workflows.
anti_patterns: Shipping a generic chat interface without domain-specific workflow value.
Ignoring API costs until real users create unexpected bills.
Trusting AI output without parsing, validation, fallback handling, or review paths.

faq.question: What kind of AI products does this skill support?
faq.answer: It supports focused products that use AI APIs inside a specific workflow, such as writing, summarization, analysis, or automation tools.

faq.question: Does this skill generate a full application?
faq.answer: No. It provides architecture patterns, implementation examples, and product guidance that engineers adapt into an application.

faq.question: Can it help with OpenAI and Anthropic APIs?
faq.answer: Yes. The guidance is written for products that wrap AI APIs, including OpenAI, Anthropic, and similar model providers.

faq.question: Does it include cost-management advice?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers usage tracking, token economics, cheaper model selection, caching, output limits, and customer usage limits.

faq.question: How does it improve output quality?
faq.answer: It recommends prompt examples, structured output formats, parsing, validation, retry logic, fallback models, and post-processing.

faq.question: Is this skill safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. The reviewed file is documentation only, and the static security alerts were false positives from Markdown and example code.
